This gives you options to ride it as a single fin, standard thruster or with a 6" center fin and side bytes, whatever's the most fun for you! 6.0" Fiberglass Center Fin and 2 Future Side Fins included. 2 + 1

description: The Ultimate 9'0 Surfboard! The Redline11 turns like an 8'6", Paddles like a 9'6", Noserides like a 10'0". Bill says this is the "best all-around longboard he's ever designed". The nose bottom has a deep single concave that continues back past the half way point. This concave is what makes the Redline 11 paddle, turn and nose ride exceptionally great. That single concave flows into a double concave with a heavy V in the rounded pin tail, which makes it loose, fast and easy to ride. Bill invented the beveled rail over 30 years ago, and it remains a key feature in many of his board designs for a reason. The bevels in the Redline 11 rails lift the rail edge making it really forgiving on the drop-in and in rail to rail turns. The hard edges in the tail rails release water for insane speed. The included 2+1 fin set-up (another Bill Stewart invention) features 2 short wide rail fins forward giving the board drive while keeping it loose. The larger center back fin holds the board in the pocket when riding up on the nose. 6.5" Fiberglass center fin and Futures composite side byte fin set included. Board # 129406 2 + 1 Length - 9'0" Width - 23 3/4" Thickness - 3 1/8"

The long, impeccably blended concaves complement the rocker in creating a board that paddles insane, allowing you to pick off that big set wave with ease. Hang on tight as the deep double concaves in the tail make the board turn like a barrel-racer – look where you wanna go and the Wild Bill's there. The beveled rails allow for more foam where you need it while having a nice thin rail to carve into those turns. Draw new lines at your local break or explore unfamiliar horizons on the Wild Bill. Tested from Hawaii to California, France to Portugal, and beyond, it works great in a variety of conditions from mediocre mushy beach breaks to steep barreling point breaks. This trusty steed will take your surfing to the next level and will quickly become your go-to. Futures tri-fin set included. Board # 129334 Thruster Length - 6'2" Width - 20 3/4" Thickness - 2 5/8"

description: Take the Redline 11 and shrink it down and you get a really versatile mini longboard that you can run to the tip for a cheater five, then back to crank turns off the tail. The Funline 11 paddles really big, rides really small. It's designed to easily catch waves in all conditions, for surfers from beginners to advanced. A mini version of our best-selling Redline 11 longboard, the Funline is super wide in the front for paddle power. It has a flat straight entry rocker with flip in the tip. An extra long concave in the nose, for trim speed and paddling, that's perfectly blended into double concave V in the tail for speed and quick rail exchange. Bill invented the beveled rail over 30 years ago, and it remains a key feature in many of his board designs for a reason. The bevels in the nose rails lift the rail edge making them really forgiving on the drop-in and in rail to rail turns. The hard edges in the tails rail release water for insane speed. Fins included! Boards under 7'6 - Futures composite thruster fin set. Boards 7'6 and over - 6.0" Fiberglass center fin and Futures composite side byte fin set included. Board # 129350 2 + 1 Length - 8'0" Width - 23" Thickness - 3"

description: Can you have too much fun? We think not! Max out your fun with the newest addition to the Stewart mid-length offering, the 2FUN. With all the mind-blowing features of the Hydro Hull egg shape, just pumped up a notch. Lower entry rocker makes paddling a breeze and the beveled rails offer forgiveness on the drop-in and in your turns. The more-dramatic vee in the tail makes this mid-length go rail to rail better than ever. Adding to the versatility of the 2FUN, we gave it a 2+1 fin setup, with a medium center fin box. This gives you options to ride it as a single fin, standard thruster or with a 6" center fin and side bytes, whatever's the most fun for you! The 2FUN really is fun for all -- it works great in just about any wave conditions and for a wide range of surfers from beginners to advanced. 6.0" Fiberglass Center Fin and 2 Future Side Fins included. Board # 129394 2 + 1 Length - 7'0" Width - 22" Thickness - 2 3/4"

description: The (949) Comp is a modified (949) with a narrower, slightly more dramatically rockered nose for easier duck-diving and dropping in to steeper faced waves. It's still got most of the longboard paddle-power of the original (949) and rides like a super responsive shortboard with its deep vee concave bottom, which makes it feel much smaller rail to rail. Designed to be surfed with the included Futures Vector 2 Quad Scimitar fins and a center 3.25" trailer fin, which give it solid bite and insane drive down the line. A definite game-changer for beach breaks, point breaks, and surf trips, this "executive shortboard" is a mind-blowing addition to your quiver. 5-fin set included. Board # 129390 5 Fin Length - 7'0" Width - 22" Thickness - 2 3/4"

description: Have you heard… about the Bird!?! Soaring on to the scene as Bill Stewart's latest longboard creation, the Bird has the grace of a classic single fin, with the power, speed, and maneuverability of a more high-performance longboard. Created for our team riders to push the envelope of classic longboarding, Bill made sure that the Bird checks all the classic log boxes for those pesky contest rules while adding some key features to make it ride way better than your average single fin. The more traditional round foiled rails towards the nose blend perfectly into hard edges off the tail, which release water for speed. Deep vee in the tail makes the wide tail go rail to rail with ease. Projecting out of turns with speed and quickly setting the Bird on rail makes maxing out your maneuvers on each wave a breeze. After you've crushed a few turns, stall for a soft landing in the pocket – the Bird's tail has plenty of width and the ideal rocker for solid hold while you perch on the nose. Speaking of the tail, you just can't help but notice the unique tail shape on this one. Mirroring a bird's wings, the design doesn't really have a functional purpose but, damn it looks good! Whether you're looking for the highest scores from the judges, or bragging rights with your camera rewind, the Bird is the word. Exclusive Bird fiberglass fin included. Board # 129359 Single Length - 9'2" Width - 23 1/4" Thickness - 3 1/8"

description: The Ultimate 9'0 Surfboard! The Redline11 turns like an 8'6", Paddles like a 9'6", Noserides like a 10'0". Bill says this is the "best all-around longboard he's ever designed". The nose bottom has a deep single concave that continues back past the half way point. This concave is what makes the Redline 11 paddle, turn and nose ride exceptionally great. That single concave flows into a double concave with a heavy V in the rounded pin tail, which makes it loose, fast and easy to ride. Bill invented the beveled rail over 30 years ago, and it remains a key feature in many of his board designs for a reason. The bevels in the Redline 11 rails lift the rail edge making it really forgiving on the drop-in and in rail to rail turns. The hard edges in the tail rails release water for insane speed. The included 2+1 fin set-up (another Bill Stewart invention) features 2 short wide rail fins forward giving the board drive while keeping it loose. The larger center back fin holds the board in the pocket when riding up on the nose. 6.5" Fiberglass center fin and Futures composite side byte fin set included. Board # 129313 2 + 1 Length - 9'0" Width - 23" Thickness - 3 1/8"

After you've crushed a few turns, stall for a soft landing in the pocket – the Bird's tail has plenty of width and the ideal rocker for solid hold while you perch on the nose. Speaking of the tail, you just can't help but notice the unique tail shape on this one. Mirroring a bird's wings, the design doesn't really have a functional purpose but, damn it looks good! Exclusive Bird fiberglass fin included.

description: The Ultimate 9'0 Surfboard! The Redline11 turns like an 8'6", Paddles like a 9'6", Noserides like a 10'0". Bill says this is the "best all-around longboard he's ever designed". The nose bottom has a deep single concave that continues back past the half way point. This concave is what makes the Redline 11 paddle, turn and nose ride exceptionally great. That single concave flows into a double concave with a heavy V in the rounded pin tail, which makes it loose, fast and easy to ride. Bill invented the beveled rail over 30 years ago, and it remains a key feature in many of his board designs for a reason. The bevels in the Redline 11 rails lift the rail edge making it really forgiving on the drop-in and in rail to rail turns. The hard edges in the tail rails release water for insane speed. The included 2+1 fin set-up (another Bill Stewart invention) features 2 short wide rail fins forward giving the board drive while keeping it loose. The larger center back fin holds the board in the pocket when riding up on the nose. 6.5" Fiberglass center fin and Futures composite side byte fin set included. Board # 129312 2 + 1 Length - 9'0" Width - 23" Thickness - 3 1/8"

description: The best of both worlds, the Ripster combines classic styling with modern technical design features, making it the ultimate performance single fin! Nose A deep, long blended concave in the nose makes this board paddle with maximum glide to catch every wave. It also creates trim speed and lift down the line, beating sections like you won't believe! Rails The rails are down hard in the tail, then soften as they go forward, making this board turn incredibly well while also being forgiving. The hard edges on the tail release water, giving it ripping speed, and the foil holds it in the pocket. Tail The heavy V in the tail runs up to the middle of the board, where it's perfectly transitioned into the nose concave. This makes rail to rail surfing easier, and beautiful back-to-back roundhouse turns effortless. Single Fin Clean & simple raked exclusive Ripster single fin, for that classic look and optimal performance for this model. Fiberglass fin included. Board # 128719 Single Length - 9'2" Width - 23 1/2" Thickness - 3"

description: Bill has tapped into his wild side and created the Wild Bill, the ultimate high-performance mid-length surfboard. For intermediate to advanced surfers, this board is a perfect alternative to your shortboard for those days that you want a bit more paddle power but don't want to sacrifice performance. The Wild Bill has the perfect rocker, hitting that magic balance between low entry rocker for paddle power while still having enough rocker to not stuff the nose on late drops. The long, impeccably blended concaves complement the rocker in creating a board that paddles insane, allowing you to pick off that big set wave with ease. Hang on tight as the deep double concaves in the tail make the board turn like a barrel-racer – look where you wanna go and the Wild Bill's there. The beveled rails allow for more foam where you need it while having a nice thin rail to carve into those turns. Draw new lines at your local break or explore unfamiliar horizons on the Wild Bill. Tested from Hawaii to California, France to Portugal, and beyond, it works great in a variety of conditions from mediocre mushy beach breaks to steep barreling point breaks. This trusty steed will take your surfing to the next level and will quickly become your go-to. Futures tri-fin set included. Board # 129319 Thruster Length - 6'6" Width - 21 1/2" Thickness - 2 3/4"

description: The Fartknocker is BACK, baby! One of our most popular shortboards of all time has been reborn as a new and improved Silent But Deadly version. With the same wide outline as the original Fartknocker, the SBD takes it up a notch with a thinner, more pulled in nose and tuned up foiled rails. The result is a stealth little high-performance groveler that'll sneak up on you and surprise you with its potency - whew, this baby will turn some heads! Not only does it permeate in slop and medium size waves, the SBD can handle bigger surf, too. The Fartknocker SBD is the perfect board for those mushy days when you're questioning if it's even worth going out. The pancake flat rocker in the midsection with a slight kick in the nose and tail make it absolutely fly down the line, crop dusting the crowd as you go. And it loves to blast aerial tricks in the smallest of waves. The Hydro Hull beveled rails put plenty of foam where you need it, giving the SBD surprising wave-catching ability for a small board. The huge single to double concave bottom make this board easy to ride, forgiving and generally super rippable. It's the kind of board that seems to know what you wanna do -- think turn and it's already there. Equipped with 5 fin boxes for versatility to ride it as a quad or a twin + trailer. Your choice of Futures composite fin set included. Board # 129179 5 Fin Length - 5'8" Width - 20 3/4" Thickness - 2 3/8"

description: Take the Redline 11 and shrink it down and you get a really versatile mini longboard that you can run to the tip for a cheater five, then back to crank turns off the tail. The Funline 11 paddles really big, rides really small. It's designed to easily catch waves in all conditions, for surfers from beginners to advanced. A mini version of our best-selling Redline 11 longboard, the Funline is super wide in the front for paddle power. It has a flat straight entry rocker with flip in the tip. An extra long concave in the nose, for trim speed and paddling, that's perfectly blended into double concave V in the tail for speed and quick rail exchange. Bill invented the beveled rail over 30 years ago, and it remains a key feature in many of his board designs for a reason. The bevels in the nose rails lift the rail edge making them really forgiving on the drop-in and in rail to rail turns. The hard edges in the tails rail release water for insane speed. Fins included! Boards under 7'6 - Futures composite thruster fin set. Boards 7'6 and over - 6.0" Fiberglass center fin and Futures composite side byte fin set included. Board # 129243 2 + 1 Length - 7'6" Width - 22 1/2" Thickness - 3"

description: A High Speed, Performance Five Fin Fun Board. The (949) has low nose entry rocker for paddle power. A monstrous deep vee concave bottom which makes the board ride much smaller rail to rail. Designed to be surfed with the included Future Vector 2 Quad Scimitar fins and a center box trailer fin, which provide solid bite and insane drive. A great board for all conditions, from punchy beach breaks to playful point breaks. You're gonna want to have this board in your quiver! 5-fin set included. Board # 129158 4 + 8" CENTER Length - 7'6" Width - 22 1/2" Thickness - 2 7/8"

description: Have you heard… about the Bird!?! Soaring on to the scene as Bill Stewart's latest longboard creation, the Bird has the grace of a classic single fin, with the power, speed, and maneuverability of a more high-performance longboard. Created for our team riders to push the envelope of classic longboarding, Bill made sure that the Bird checks all the classic log boxes for those pesky contest rules while adding some key features to make it ride way better than your average single fin. The more traditional round foiled rails towards the nose blend perfectly into hard edges off the tail, which release water for speed. Deep vee in the tail makes the wide tail go rail to rail with ease. Projecting out of turns with speed and quickly setting the Bird on rail makes maxing out your maneuvers on each wave a breeze. After you've crushed a few turns, stall for a soft landing in the pocket – the Bird's tail has plenty of width and the ideal rocker for solid hold while you perch on the nose. Speaking of the tail, you just can't help but notice the unique tail shape on this one. Mirroring a bird's wings, the design doesn't really have a functional purpose but, damn it looks good! Whether you're looking for the highest scores from the judges, or bragging rights with your camera rewind, the Bird is the word. Exclusive Bird fiberglass fin included. Board # 129263 Single Length - 10'0" Width - 24" Thickness - 3 1/2"

description: Introduced in 2015, the Tipster is hands-down the Best Stewart Noseriding Longboard ever! Tested by team riders Tony Silvagni and Steven Newton, both of whom have been winning contests on it since Fall 2014. The Tipster has also been stylishly free surfed by our team on the East & West coasts. Bill Stewart calls it "Beyond Clean, Beyond Classic." The nose concave creates lift and holds you in the pocket. The bottom is flat for insane trim speed, to carry you through the flats. The huge square tail has rocker you won't believe till you see it! That extra tail kick makes turning easy and sets you up for endless nose rides!!! Exclusive Tipster fiberglass noseriding fin included. Board # 128858 Single Length - 9'6" Width - 23 1/2" Thickness - 3 1/4"

Hang on tight as the deep double concaves in the tail make the board turn like a barrel-racer – look where you wanna go and the Wild Bill's there. The beveled rails allow for more foam where you need it while having a nice thin rail to carve into those turns. Draw new lines at your local break or explore unfamiliar horizons on the Wild Bill. Tested from Hawaii to California, France to Portugal, and beyond, it works great in a variety of conditions from mediocre mushy beach breaks to steep barreling point breaks. This trusty steed will take your surfing to the next level and will quickly become your go-to. Futures tri-fin set included. Board # 129147 Thruster Length - 6'6" Width - 21" Thickness - 2 5/8"

description: Have you heard… about the Bird!?! Soaring on to the scene as Bill Stewart's latest longboard creation, the Bird has the grace of a classic single fin, with the power, speed, and maneuverability of a more high-performance longboard. Created for our team riders to push the envelope of classic longboarding, Bill made sure that the Bird checks all the classic log boxes for those pesky contest rules while adding some key features to make it ride way better than your average single fin. The more traditional round foiled rails towards the nose blend perfectly into hard edges off the tail, which release water for speed. Deep vee in the tail makes the wide tail go rail to rail with ease. Projecting out of turns with speed and quickly setting the Bird on rail makes maxing out your maneuvers on each wave a breeze. After you've crushed a few turns, stall for a soft landing in the pocket – the Bird's tail has plenty of width and the ideal rocker for solid hold while you perch on the nose. Speaking of the tail, you just can't help but notice the unique tail shape on this one. Mirroring a bird's wings, the design doesn't really have a functional purpose but, damn it looks good! Whether you're looking for the highest scores from the judges, or bragging rights with your camera rewind, the Bird is the word. Exclusive Bird fiberglass fin included. Board # 128877 Single Length - 10'0" Width - 24" Thickness - 3 1/2"

description: The Ultimate 9'0 Surfboard! The Redline11 turns like an 8'6", Paddles like a 9'6", Noserides like a 10'0". Bill says this is the "best all-around longboard he's ever designed". The nose bottom has a deep single concave that continues back past the half way point. This concave is what makes the Redline 11 paddle, turn and nose ride exceptionally great. That single concave flows into a double concave with a heavy V in the rounded pin tail, which makes it loose, fast and easy to ride. Bill invented the beveled rail over 30 years ago, and it remains a key feature in many of his board designs for a reason. The bevels in the Redline 11 rails lift the rail edge making it really forgiving on the drop-in and in rail to rail turns. The hard edges in the tail rails release water for insane speed. The included 2+1 fin set-up (another Bill Stewart invention) features 2 short wide rail fins forward giving the board drive while keeping it loose. The larger center back fin holds the board in the pocket when riding up on the nose. 6.5" Fiberglass center fin and Futures composite side byte fin set included. Board # 129078 2 + 1 Length - 9'0" Width - 24 1/2" Thickness - 3 1/8"

description: Take the Redline 11 and shrink it down and you get a really versatile mini longboard that you can run to the tip for a cheater five, then back to crank turns off the tail. The Funline 11 paddles really big, rides really small. It's designed to easily catch waves in all conditions, for surfers from beginners to advanced. A mini version of our best-selling Redline 11 longboard, the Funline is super wide in the front for paddle power. It has a flat straight entry rocker with flip in the tip. An extra long concave in the nose, for trim speed and paddling, that's perfectly blended into double concave V in the tail for speed and quick rail exchange. Bill invented the beveled rail over 30 years ago, and it remains a key feature in many of his board designs for a reason. The bevels in the nose rails lift the rail edge making them really forgiving on the drop-in and in rail to rail turns. The hard edges in the tails rail release water for insane speed. Fins included! Boards under 7'6 - Futures composite thruster fin set. Boards 7'6 and over - 6.0" Fiberglass center fin and Futures composite side byte fin set included. Board # 129029 Thruster Length - 7'0" Width - 22" Thickness - 3"

description: The Retro Fish is an updated version of a timeless classic. The ample volume throughout this board provides you with the paddle power, float, and speed you need to zip through flat sections in fatter waves. Because it's got so much volume, you wanna go about 4"- 6" shorter than your standard shortboard. Quad fin set included, which gives the Retro Fish more drive with a looser feel versus the drawn-out turns of the old standard keel fin fishes. This fun and classic design is a great addition to anyone's quiver! Futures composite quad fin set included. Board # 116347 Quad Length - 5'10" Width - 2 7/8" Thickness - 2 3/4"

description: The Fartknocker is BACK, baby! One of our most popular shortboards of all time has been reborn as a new and improved Silent But Deadly version. With the same wide outline as the original Fartknocker, the SBD takes it up a notch with a thinner, more pulled in nose and tuned up foiled rails. The result is a stealth little high-performance groveler that'll sneak up on you and surprise you with its potency - whew, this baby will turn some heads! Not only does it permeate in slop and medium size waves, the SBD can handle bigger surf, too. The Fartknocker SBD is the perfect board for those mushy days when you're questioning if it's even worth going out. The pancake flat rocker in the midsection with a slight kick in the nose and tail make it absolutely fly down the line, crop dusting the crowd as you go. And it loves to blast aerial tricks in the smallest of waves. The Hydro Hull beveled rails put plenty of foam where you need it, giving the SBD surprising wave-catching ability for a small board. The huge single to double concave bottom make this board easy to ride, forgiving and generally super rippable. It's the kind of board that seems to know what you wanna do -- think turn and it's already there. Equipped with 5 fin boxes for versatility to ride it as a quad or a twin + trailer. Your choice of Futures composite fin set included. Board # 128847 5 Fin Length - 6'0" Width - 21 1/4" Thickness - 2 1/2"

The bevels in the Redline 11 rails lift the rail edge making it really forgiving on the drop-in and in rail to rail turns. The hard edges in the tail rails release water for insane speed. The included 2+1 fin set-up (another Bill Stewart invention) features 2 short wide rail fins forward giving the board drive while keeping it loose. The larger center back fin holds the board in the pocket when riding up on the nose. 6.5" Fiberglass center fin and Futures composite side byte fin set included. 2 + 1

description: Take the Redline 11 and shrink it down and you get a really versatile mini longboard that you can run to the tip for a cheater five, then back to crank turns off the tail. The Funline 11 paddles really big, rides really small. It's designed to easily catch waves in all conditions, for surfers from beginners to advanced. A mini version of our best-selling Redline 11 longboard, the Funline is super wide in the front for paddle power. It has a flat straight entry rocker with flip in the tip. An extra long concave in the nose, for trim speed and paddling, that's perfectly blended into double concave V in the tail for speed and quick rail exchange. Bill invented the beveled rail over 30 years ago, and it remains a key feature in many of his board designs for a reason. The bevels in the nose rails lift the rail edge making them really forgiving on the drop-in and in rail to rail turns. The hard edges in the tails rail release water for insane speed. Fins included! Boards under 7'6 - Futures composite thruster fin set. Boards 7'6 and over - 6.0" Fiberglass center fin and Futures composite side byte fin set included. Board # 128756 Thruster Length - 7'0" Width - 22" Thickness - 3"

description: The Fartknocker is BACK, baby! One of our most popular shortboards of all time has been reborn as a new and improved Silent But Deadly version. With the same wide outline as the original Fartknocker, the SBD takes it up a notch with a thinner, more pulled in nose and tuned up foiled rails. The result is a stealth little high-performance groveler that'll sneak up on you and surprise you with its potency - whew, this baby will turn some heads! Not only does it permeate in slop and medium size waves, the SBD can handle bigger surf, too. The Fartknocker SBD is the perfect board for those mushy days when you're questioning if it's even worth going out. The pancake flat rocker in the midsection with a slight kick in the nose and tail make it absolutely fly down the line, crop dusting the crowd as you go. And it loves to blast aerial tricks in the smallest of waves. The Hydro Hull beveled rails put plenty of foam where you need it, giving the SBD surprising wave-catching ability for a small board. The huge single to double concave bottom make this board easy to ride, forgiving and generally super rippable. It's the kind of board that seems to know what you wanna do -- think turn and it's already there. Equipped with 5 fin boxes for versatility to ride it as a quad or a twin + trailer. Your choice of Futures composite fin set included. Board # 128625 5 Fin Length - 5'2" Width - 20" Thickness - 2 1/4"

description: Introduced in 2015, the Tipster is hands-down the Best Stewart Noseriding Longboard ever! Tested by team riders Tony Silvagni and Steven Newton, both of whom have been winning contests on it since Fall 2014. The Tipster has also been stylishly free surfed by our team on the East & West coasts. Bill Stewart calls it "Beyond Clean, Beyond Classic." The nose concave creates lift and holds you in the pocket. The bottom is flat for insane trim speed, to carry you through the flats. The huge square tail has rocker you won't believe till you see it! That extra tail kick makes turning easy and sets you up for endless nose rides!!! Exclusive Tipster fiberglass noseriding fin included. Board # 128578 Single Length - 9'0" Width - 23" Thickness - 3"

description: Can you have too much fun? We think not! Max out your fun with the newest addition to the Stewart mid-length offering, the 2FUN. With all the mind-blowing features of the Hydro Hull egg shape, just pumped up a notch. Lower entry rocker makes paddling a breeze and the beveled rails offer forgiveness on the drop-in and in your turns. The more-dramatic vee in the tail makes this mid-length go rail to rail better than ever. Adding to the versatility of the 2FUN, we gave it a 2+1 fin setup, with a medium center fin box. This gives you options to ride it as a single fin, standard thruster or with a 6" center fin and side bytes, whatever's the most fun for you! The 2FUN really is fun for all -- it works great in just about any wave conditions and for a wide range of surfers from beginners to advanced. 6.0" Fiberglass Center Fin and 2 Future Side Fins included. Board # 128485 2 + 1 Length - 6'6" Width - 21 1/2" Thickness - 2 3/4"

description: Bill has tapped into his wild side and created the Wild Bill, the ultimate high-performance mid-length surfboard. For intermediate to advanced surfers, this board is a perfect alternative to your shortboard for those days that you want a bit more paddle power but don't want to sacrifice performance. The Wild Bill has the perfect rocker, hitting that magic balance between low entry rocker for paddle power while still having enough rocker to not stuff the nose on late drops. The long, impeccably blended concaves complement the rocker in creating a board that paddles insane, allowing you to pick off that big set wave with ease. Hang on tight as the deep double concaves in the tail make the board turn like a barrel-racer – look where you wanna go and the Wild Bill's there. The beveled rails allow for more foam where you need it while having a nice thin rail to carve into those turns. Draw new lines at your local break or explore unfamiliar horizons on the Wild Bill. Tested from Hawaii to California, France to Portugal, and beyond, it works great in a variety of conditions from mediocre mushy beach breaks to steep barreling point breaks. This trusty steed will take your surfing to the next level and will quickly become your go-to. Board # 127286 Thruster Length - 6'0 Width - 20 1/2" Thickness - 2 1/2"

description: The best of both worlds, the Ripster combines classic styling with modern technical design features, making it the ultimate performance single fin! Nose A deep, long blended concave in the nose makes this board paddle with maximum glide to catch every wave. It also creates trim speed and lift down the line, beating sections like you won't believe! Rails The rails are down hard in the tail, then soften as they go forward, making this board turn incredibly well while also being forgiving. The hard edges on the tail release water, giving it ripping speed, and the foil holds it in the pocket. Tail The heavy V in the tail runs up to the middle of the board, where it's perfectly transitioned into the nose concave. This makes rail to rail surfing easier, and beautiful back-to-back roundhouse turns effortless. Single Fin Clean & simple raked exclusive Ripster single fin, for that classic look and optimal performance for this model. Fiberglass fin included. Board # 128443 Single Length - 9'8" Width - 24" Thickness - 3 3/8"

description: Have you heard… about the Bird!?! Soaring on to the scene as Bill Stewart's latest longboard creation, the Bird has the grace of a classic single fin, with the power, speed, and maneuverability of a more high-performance longboard. Created for our team riders to push the envelope of classic longboarding, Bill made sure that the Bird checks all the classic log boxes for those pesky contest rules while adding some key features to make it ride way better than your average single fin. The more traditional round foiled rails towards the nose blend perfectly into hard edges off the tail, which release water for speed. Deep vee in the tail makes the wide tail go rail to rail with ease. Projecting out of turns with speed and quickly setting the Bird on rail makes maxing out your maneuvers on each wave a breeze. After you've crushed a few turns, stall for a soft landing in the pocket – the Bird's tail has plenty of width and the ideal rocker for solid hold while you perch on the nose. Speaking of the tail, you just can't help but notice the unique tail shape on this one. Mirroring a bird's wings, the design doesn't really have a functional purpose but, damn it looks good! Whether you're looking for the highest scores from the judges, or bragging rights with your camera rewind, the Bird is the word. Exclusive Bird fiberglass fin included. Board # 128398 Single Length - 9'4" Width - 23 1/2" Thickness - 3 1/4"

Created for our team riders to push the envelope of classic longboarding, Bill made sure that the Bird checks all the classic log boxes for those pesky contest rules while adding some key features to make it ride way better than your average single fin. The more traditional round foiled rails towards the nose blend perfectly into hard edges off the tail, which release water for speed. Deep vee in the tail makes the wide tail go rail to rail with ease. Projecting out of turns with speed and quickly setting the Bird on rail makes maxing out your maneuvers on each wave a breeze. After you've crushed a few turns, stall for a soft landing in the pocket – the Bird's tail has plenty of width and the ideal rocker for solid hold while you perch on the nose. Speaking of the tail, you just can't help but notice the unique tail shape on this one. Mirroring a bird's wings, the design doesn't really have a functional purpose but, damn it looks good! Exclusive Bird fiberglass fin included.

description: The Ultimate 9'0 Surfboard! The Redline11 turns like an 8'6", Paddles like a 9'6", Noserides like a 10'0". Bill says this is the "best all-around longboard he's ever designed". The nose bottom has a deep single concave that continues back past the half way point. This concave is what makes the Redline 11 paddle, turn and nose ride exceptionally great. That single concave flows into a double concave with a heavy V in the rounded pin tail, which makes it loose, fast and easy to ride. Bill invented the beveled rail over 30 years ago, and it remains a key feature in many of his board designs for a reason. The bevels in the Redline 11 rails lift the rail edge making it really forgiving on the drop-in and in rail to rail turns. The hard edges in the tail rails release water for insane speed. The included 2+1 fin set-up (another Bill Stewart invention) features 2 short wide rail fins forward giving the board drive while keeping it loose. The larger center back fin holds the board in the pocket when riding up on the nose. 6.5" Fiberglass center fin and Futures composite side byte fin set included. Board # 128328 2 + 1 Length - 9'0" Width - 23" Thickness - 3"

description: The Retro Fish is an updated version of a timeless classic. The ample volume throughout this board provides you with the paddle power, float, and speed you need to zip through flat sections in fatter waves. Because it's got so much volume, you wanna go about 4"- 6" shorter than your standard shortboard. Quad fin set included, which gives the Retro Fish more drive with a looser feel versus the drawn-out turns of the old standard keel fin fishes. This fun and classic design is a great addition to anyone's quiver! Futures composite quad fin set included. Board # 128281 Quad Length - 5'8" Width - 20 1/2 Thickness - 2 1/2

description: *Discounted due to extremely slight yellowing. Cosmetic issue only. Can you have too much fun? We think not! Max out your fun with the newest addition to the Stewart mid-length offering, the 2FUN. With all the mind-blowing features of the Hydro Hull egg shape, just pumped up a notch. Lower entry rocker makes paddling a breeze and the beveled rails offer forgiveness on the drop-in and in your turns. The more-dramatic vee in the tail makes this mid-length go rail to rail better than ever. Adding to the versatility of the 2FUN, we gave it a 2+1 fin setup, with a medium center fin box. This gives you options to ride it as a single fin, standard thruster or with a 6" center fin and side bytes, whatever's the most fun for you! The 2FUN really is fun for all -- it works great in just about any wave conditions and for a wide range of surfers from beginners to advanced. 6.0" Fiberglass Center Fin and 2 Future Side Fins included. Board # 127858 2 + 1 Length - 7'0" Width - 22" Thickness - 2 3/4"

description: Bill has tapped into his wild side and created the Wild Bill, the ultimate high-performance mid-length surfboard. For intermediate to advanced surfers, this board is a perfect alternative to your shortboard for those days that you want a bit more paddle power but don't want to sacrifice performance. The Wild Bill has the perfect rocker, hitting that magic balance between low entry rocker for paddle power while still having enough rocker to not stuff the nose on late drops. The long, impeccably blended concaves complement the rocker in creating a board that paddles insane, allowing you to pick off that big set wave with ease. Hang on tight as the deep double concaves in the tail make the board turn like a barrel-racer – look where you wanna go and the Wild Bill's there. The beveled rails allow for more foam where you need it while having a nice thin rail to carve into those turns. Draw new lines at your local break or explore unfamiliar horizons on the Wild Bill. Tested from Hawaii to California, France to Portugal, and beyond, it works great in a variety of conditions from mediocre mushy beach breaks to steep barreling point breaks. This trusty steed will take your surfing to the next level and will quickly become your go-to. Futures tri-fin set included. Board # 127678 Thruster Length - 6'0 Width - 20 1/2" Thickness - 2 1/2"
